title: spiranthes ovalis var. erostellata
intro: Spiranthes ovalis var. erostellata, also known as Eastern Ladies' Tresses, is a type of orchid that belongs to the Siphoglossum genus and is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of phenanthrenes and other compounds. The health benefits of Eastern Ladies' Tresses include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as a tincture, powder, or tea, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
